title: Gnome Remote Desktop (Fedora 40 Workstation)
author: r-w-frederick
body_snippet:
## Expected Behavior
Attempt to connect to remote Gnome Remote Desktop (Fedora 40 Workstation) with RDP protocol from Windows client.
Expect behavior similar to remote connection to Windows via RDP, and remote Linux hosts via RDP/XRPD.

## Current Behavior
Supplying Username & Password leads to error.
Error code: 0x0
Extended error code: 0x0

Remove UN/PW from mRemoteNG config pops Windows Security dialog.  Supply correct UN/PW.
GNOME Remote Desktop Handover Daemon complains:
"This co...

iterations.description: Issue lacks reproducible steps and expected vs actual behavior for connecting to Gnome Remote Desktop on Fedora 40; needs confirmation whether the problem persists on latest build and what specific RDP protocol negotiation failure occurs.
Wontfix: MSTSCLib COM reference upgrade risks build breakage on older Windows Server versions

comments.author: fuzzzerd
comments.snippet: This property is not implemented as a setting; however, the default RDP protocol uses [`MsRdpClient6NotSafeForScripting`](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/termserv/msrdpclient6notsafeforscripting) which does NOT have [UseRedirectionServerName](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/termserv/imsrdppreferredredirectioninfo-useredirectionservername); as this was implemented in [`MsRdpClient7NotSafeForScripting`](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/termserv/msrdpc...

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet:
> Perhaps this needs to be implemented for the higher versions of RDP Protocol that support it?

we had this for windows 7 support, but as Microsoft stop supporting it I think we could drop it to. So far thinking to add in settings what protocol version to use and by default that will be hiest posible, not lowest as per now

notes: Feature request: Gnome Remote Desktop (RDP alternative). Requires new protocol plugin.
